It currently computes `x + result`, allocating a NEW full-size tensor each call and writing the sum into it. `result = f(...).view(x.shape)` is always a freshly-produced, exclusively-owned tensor (Linear/out-projection output), so `result.add_(x)` writes the residual in-place into that buffer — bit-identical to `x + result` (same elementwise fp add, same order) but eliminating one full-tensor allocation + the associated memory traffic per sublayer in this launch/copy-bound regime (aten::copy_/add are top GPU ops). Guarded by torch.is_grad_enabled() so training/autograd keeps the exact out-of-place `x + result` (no in-place mutation of a tensor needed for backward), making the change inference-only and output-preserving. x itself is never mutated (add_ targets result), so no aliasing hazard.

The accepted group-compile of the stage-3 ICL block loop (+3.5%) only runs on the `sample` path because `enable_torch_compile` is forced False for <8192 rows; the `typical` path runs all 24 ICLTransformerBlocks fully EAGER as a per-block launch storm (RMSNorm/projection/SDPA/MLP), and `typical` is the MOST launch-bound regime (GPU 25-60% busy) — exactly where fusing the storm pays most. Prior typical-compile rejections were whole-stack-as-one-graph, per-block (dispatch boundaries), or FULL compile incl. stages 0-2 (recompile thrash). This is materially different: reuse the EXACT proven group-compile mechanism (`_run_icl_group` via `self._compiled`, groups of 6 → 4 graphs compiled once and reused across groups + repeated predicts), but extend its gate to also cover the eager typical path (cuda, not force_recompute, not compiling), leaving stages 0-2 eager. num_train stays a static python-int guard; x_BRiD batch/row dims are mark_dynamic so varying test-row counts don't recompile. Output-preserving: same Inductor kernels, only op-fusion/fp-reordering the argmax+JS gate already tolerates on the compiled path. The compile cache (self._torch_compile_cache) is already popped in __getstate__. Expected: large launch-overhead reduction on the launch-bound typical point; sample point unchanged (already takes this branch).

In ManyClassDecoder's _chunked_class_attention, the V class axis is split into head_dim-sized chunks, the chunk index is folded into the batch dim, and q/k/v are expand+reshape+.contiguous()'d before a single flash call. When num_chunks == 1 (max_num_classes <= head_dim, the common case), this folding is a pure identity: q_BSHD, k_BJHD are already (B,S/J,H,D)-contiguous projection outputs and v_BJHT is contiguous after the (≤D) pad. The unsqueeze(1).expand(-1,1,...).reshape(...).contiguous() chain therefore just issues full-tensor copy kernels (aten::copy_, the #1 GPU op) — most importantly q_folded.contiguous(), whose sequence dim S is the test-row count (large on the sample point). Add a num_chunks==1 fast path that calls _batched_scaled_dot_product_attention directly on q_BSHD/k_BJHD/v_BJHT (all last-dim stride 1 → flash still selected, same backend, same math) and trims the padding via [...,:T]. Output is bit-identical (same SDPA, same softmax-scaling layer, same padding-trim); only the redundant fold/copy kernels are removed. Mechanism: fewer large copy kernels + bytes moved in the decoder, mirroring the accepted SDPA-.contiguous() removal win, helping the copy-bound regime.

Host-overhead cut in the launch/CPU-bound regime (GPU only 25-60% busy, so wall time is dominated by Python+dispatch). `_fused_projection_weight` is invoked on EVERY attention projection (Attention QKV, CrossAttention KV, ICLAttention KV -> well over 100 calls/predict). On the dominant cache-HIT path it still rebuilds a fresh `(tuple(w._version for w in weights), w0.dtype, w0.device)` key (a generator, a tuple, attribute reads) and a dict comparison every single call before returning the already-cached fused weight. During inference the projection weights are frozen, so we can validate the cache with a lightweight per-weight `_version` scan (and dtype/device check) WITHOUT materialising the key tuple, returning the cached fused tensor immediately on the common hit path. This removes per-call Python allocation/comparison work across all attention calls in the eager (typical) path while remaining byte-identical (same fused weight, same fallback build on miss/invalidation; still disabled under training/compile). Mechanism: fewer host-side ops per attention call -> less CPU bottleneck in the launch-bound regime -> faster typical; sample (compiled) takes the `is_compiling()` early-return and is byte-identical/neutral.

forward_cross (ColumnAggregator's CLS-readout block) is the ONE attention site still issuing k_projection and v_projection as TWO separate GEMMs on the same input c_flat, immediately defeating the accepted fused-KV mechanism used everywhere else (CrossAttention/ICLAttention via _fused_projection_weight). Fuse them into a single F.linear with a cached concatenated weight (frozen inference weights -> cat runs once, reused across predicts), cutting one GEMM launch (addmm/mm are in the top GPU ops) per ColumnAggregator readout. The cache helper returns None under compile/training, so the sample (compiled) path and training fall back to the identical separate projections -> byte-identical graph. On the eager typical path the math is identical (same weights, just concatenated then sliced), so outputs are bit-preserved while one launch + its dispatch is removed in this launch-bound regime.

Every transformer block ends its feed-forward as `x + mlp(norm(x))`, where `mlp` is the bias-free two-layer GELU `MLP` (Linear->GELU->Linear). In the launch-bound eager (typical) regime the trailing residual add is a standalone aten::add kernel (3.08ms / 443 calls) plus a fresh allocation, issued once per ICL block (24x), per ColumnAggregator block, per dist-embedder cross-attn block, and the CLS-readout block. `torch.addmm` folds the final projection matmul and the residual add into ONE kernel (beta=1): out = residual + hidden @ W2^T. This removes one launch + one allocation per block on the host's critical path. Output-preserving: addmm computes the identical matmul (fp32 accumulation) and adds the residual before the single output rounding — a fusion-level fp reordering of exactly the kind already accepted on the typical point for compiled RMSNorm (+4.4%) and RoPE (+2.7%), not a math change; the weight remains fp32 and is autocast-cast identically to the original F.linear path. Strictly gated to the eager inference regime (save_peak_memory_factor is None, not training, not torch.compiler.is_compiling()), so the chunked/memory-saving path and the compiled+cudagraph sample path fall back to the original chunked_evaluate residual and stay byte-identical; only the eager typical path takes the fused addmm. New free function `_fused_residual_ffn` operates on a 2-D view so it serves all block shapes (B,R,E / B,R,C,E / B,Q,E). Mechanism: one fewer kernel launch + one fewer allocation per transformer block per predict in a launch-overhead-bound regime.

Under autocast(bf16)+inference_mode the per-call fp32->bf16 weight cast is NOT cached by autocast, so the LARGE fused QKV/KV projection weight (cached only in fp32 by `_fused_projection_weight`) is re-cast to bf16 on EVERY F.linear in every eager attention block (~166/predict) — aten::copy_ is the #1 GPU op (7.97ms/1886). Extend the helper with an `out_dtype` arg so it caches the fused weight ALREADY cast to the activation dtype (version/dtype/target-dtype-keyed; frozen inference weights so cat+cast run once and are reused). F.linear then receives a matching-dtype weight and autocast no-ops the cast. Bit-identical: autocast would .to(bf16)-cast the same fp32 fused weight (same round-to-nearest) then run the identical bf16 GEMM (fp32 accumulate); only the redundant per-call cast copy is removed. Gated to not-training/not-compiling (returns None there) so the compiled sample subgraphs stay byte-identical. When no autocast is active, input dtype == weight dtype so no cast happens (identical). Mechanism: removes the big per-call weight-cast copies from the launch/bandwidth-bound eager path -> fewer/cheaper copy kernels, faster typical, neutral sample.

In TransformerBlock.forward_cross (ColumnAggregator's CLS-readout last block), the query argument is always cls_part = x_full[..., :num_cls, :] — i.e. exactly the first Q tokens of the context tensor context_BRCE (the caller is ColumnAggregator.forward: cls_part = x_full[..., :self.num_cls_tokens, :]; forward_cross(cls_part, x_full, ...)). RMSNorm (_DtypeMatchingRMSNorm) normalizes each token independently over the embedding axis E, so self.layernorm(query_BRQE) is bit-identical to self.layernorm(context_BRCE)[..., :Q, :]. The current code issues TWO separate RMSNorm dispatches (one over the query, one over the full context); in this launch-bound regime that is one extra norm launch (which, via _fused_rms_norm, is a compiled-region dispatch + kernel) on every predict. Compute the context norm once and slice the query view from it, removing the redundant second normalization while producing byte-identical q_flat. Mechanism: one fewer norm dispatch/kernel per predict on both operating points; output-preserving (identical values, RMSNorm is per-token).

In ColumnAggregator's self-attention (Attention.forward, run per feat-agg block on the launch-bound eager/typical path) RoPE is applied to q and k via TWO separate `_fused_apply_rope` calls. Each call is an independent compiled-graph dispatch (guard check + kernel launch) that rebuilds the SAME data-independent positions/freqs/cos/sin chain (arange+outer-mul+cos+sin+2×cat). q and k share seq_len in self-attention, so build cos/sin ONCE and apply to both inside a SINGLE compiled graph (`_fused_apply_rope_pair`, dynamic=False), issuing one guarded dispatch per block instead of two and letting Inductor fuse the shared cos/sin + both q/k combines. Unlike the prior rejected stack-based pair (which added a cat/copy of the big q/k), this passes q and k as separate args — zero added data movement. Bit-identical: each of q,k is rotated by the exact same ops/values as before; only the duplicate cos/sin build and one dispatch are elided (fp-reorder tolerated by the gate). Gated to eager (`not training and not compiling`); the compiled/sample graph and training path keep the original two-call behaviour. Mechanism: fewer kernel launches + fewer host dispatches in the launch-bound regime → faster typical, neutral sample.

Mirror the ACCEPTED fused-QKV/KV-projection pattern (+2.1%, iter 4) into the one place it was never applied: TransformerBlock.forward_cross (ColumnAggregator's CLS-readout last block). There, k and v are produced by two SEPARATE nn.Linear GEMMs from the SAME input c_flat (shape B*R, V, E with V = num_cls + C), then sliced into heads. Concatenating the k/v weights along the output dim and issuing ONE F.linear (cached via the existing _fused_projection_weight helper, keyed on weight version/dtype/device, so the torch.cat runs once for frozen inference weights) cuts two GEMM launches to one over the largest attention input in the column aggregator. The cache attr "_fused_kv_cache" is distinct from the self-attn path's "_fused_qkv_cache", so no collision. Bit-exact: the concatenated-then-sliced matmul is the same proven transform already accepted elsewhere; falls back to separate projections under training/compile (returns None). Expected mechanism: fewer GEMM/launch dispatches on the launch-bound eager (typical) path, neutral on the compiled sample path (Inductor already fuses, and the None fallback keeps the traced graph identical).

In ColumnAggregator's CLS-readout (`TransformerBlock.forward_cross`), K and V are projected from the SAME normalized context tensor `c_flat` via two separate `nn.Linear` GEMMs — the accepted fused-QKV/KV pass (iter 4, +2.1%) never touched this method. This readout runs over a folded (B*R, V, E) tensor (B*R = number of rows, the dominant dimension on the typical path), so it is a sizeable GEMM, and the launch-bound eager regime pays the host dispatch + kernel-launch cost of two separate projections every predict. Reuse the existing `_fused_projection_weight` helper (keyed on weight version/dtype/device so the concat runs once for frozen inference weights and is reused) to evaluate K and V with a single `F.linear` against the stacked weight, then slice apart. Bit-identical: one matmul against the concatenated [k_w; v_w] equals the two separate matmuls, and the `kv[:, :, 0]`/`kv[:, :, 1]` slices reproduce the exact K/V values and shapes (`-1` == num_heads). `_fused_projection_weight` returns None under `torch.compiler.is_compiling()`/training, so the compiled sample path and any training fall back to the original two projections (identical graph). The cache attr `_fused_kv_cache` is stored on the `Attention` submodule, which only uses `_fused_qkv_cache` in its own forward, so there is no collision. Mechanism: one fewer GEMM/launch per predict in a launch-overhead-bound regime; neutral on the compiled sample point.

Sample-path ICL group loop: switch its torch.compile from dynamic=True (shared _compiled) to dynamic=False static specialization (new _compiled_static), dropping the per-group mark_dynamic on x_BRiD. Motivation mirrors the accepted _fused_rms_norm result where dynamic=False (per-shape specialization) beat dynamic=True decisively (+8% vs +0.1%): the symbolic-shape machinery of dynamic=True adds per-call guard/dispatch overhead that partly cancels the launch savings. x_BRiD has a fixed (B, num_train+num_test, D) shape across all 4 groups within a predict and across the harness's repeated same-shape sample predicts, so dynamic=False compiles each group's graph once and reuses it, letting Inductor emit static-shape (guard-light) kernels. cache_size_limit is already raised to 256, so any cross-member shape variation recompiles are bounded. Strictly gated to the enable_torch_compile branch (compile is disabled for <8192 rows), so the eager typical operating point is byte-identical and cannot regress; only the sample point changes, via op-fusion/static-shape kernels whose fp-reordering the argmax+JS gate already tolerates for the compiled path. The new static-compile cache is popped in __getstate__ for picklability like the existing one.

Sample-path decoder is the last fully-eager region. On the sample (enable_torch_compile, >=8192-row) path stages 0-2 are torch.compiled and the ICL loop is group-compiled, but `many_class_decoder` runs FULLY EAGER every predict: a launch storm of q/k Linear, one_hot+cast+contiguous, unsqueeze/expand/contiguous, F.pad, reshape/permute folds, a flash-attn call, head-mean, transpose, clamp+add+log — unfused eager launches around an attention in the GPU-underutilized regime. Route the decoder through the existing `self._compiled` machinery (dynamic=True, cached per underlying function, popped on pickle) so Inductor fuses the pointwise chain into far fewer kernels and specializes on the fixed (M,N,classes) shape reused across repeated timed predicts. The earlier decoder-compile rejection (0.9824x) compiled it on BOTH operating points, recompile-thrashing the eager typical path; this is strictly gated to `enable_torch_compile and not torch.compiler.is_compiling()`, so the eager typical path is byte-identical and untouched (cannot regress) and only the already compile-tolerant sample path is affected (fp-reordering within the argmax+JS gate). At worst a graph break makes it neutral. Expected mechanism: fewer kernel launches + fused pointwise around the decoder attention -> faster sample, neutral typical.

ManyClassDecoder.forward runs ONCE per predict on both operating points and is a launch storm: q/k Linear projections, F.one_hot, several view/contiguous, _chunked_class_attention (pad+fold+SDPA+unfold), a head-mean, and log/clamp — dozens of tiny kernels. On the EAGER (typical) path it currently runs fully eager (prior notes only tried compiling the decoder on the SAMPLE path, where it was neutral/no-speedup — the typical/eager path was never compiled). I move the body into `_forward_impl` and add a dispatcher `forward` that routes through ONE lazily-`torch.compile`d region (dynamic=True so typical & sample test-row counts share a graph; cache_size_limit is already raised elsewhere) when not training, not already compiling, and M>0 (M==0 keeps the eager dummy-grad path since flash rejects a 0-length query). Inductor fuses the pointwise/view chain and reduces host launches in the 25-60%-GPU-busy regime. Output-preserving: same ops, only fp-reordering from fusion (within gate tolerance); the softmax-scaling base_scales cache and SDPA backend context already key off torch.compiler.is_compiling() so behavior inside the graph matches the sample path. Expected: faster typical, neutral sample.

On the eager (typical) inference path, FeatureDistributionEmbedder's fast path runs each InducedSelfAttentionBlock._induced_attention EAGERLY in a per-layer loop (×num_layers, over the folded (B*C, Ri, E) tensor) — a launch storm of RMSNorm/projection/SDPA/MLP tiny kernels that is pure overhead in the launch-bound regime (GPU only 25-60% busy). Prior typical-path block-compile attempts targeted the ICL loop and "stages 0-2" as a whole; the dist-embedder induced-attention body specifically has not been compiled. Mirror the ACCEPTED sample-path block-group compile (+3.5%) and the _fused_rms_norm/_fused_apply_rope per-function precedents (+4.4%/+2.7%): compile the pure _induced_attention body ONCE and replay a fused, guard-light graph across all layers/column-folds and repeated predicts. dynamic=True + mark_dynamic on the folded-batch and row dims keeps recompiles bounded as row counts vary. Gated to the existing eager fast path (not training, not compiling, no chunking), so the compiled sample path is byte-identical/untouched and only takes the eager fallback. Output-preserving: identical math (the constant-query cache is simply bypassed under is_compiling and recomputed bit-identically); only op-fusion/fp-reordering the argmax+JS gate tolerates. Expected mechanism: many fewer kernel launches on the typical path → faster typical; neutral sample.

Scoped norm+MLP fusion for the ICL blocks only. In ColumnAggregator's per-block self-attention (Attention.forward, run on the launch-bound eager/typical path), RoPE is applied to q and k via TWO separate dispatches into the compiled _fused_apply_rope region (q.transpose then k.transpose), i.e. two full compiled-region launches per block. q and k have identical shape (B,S,H,D) here, so stacking them along a new leading dim and rotating once issues ONE compiled RoPE dispatch instead of two, halving the per-block rope launch count in this launch/overhead-bound regime (GPU 25-60% busy). Distinct from the prior rejected cos/sin-sharing edit (which kept two separate combine dispatches); this collapses to a single fused-region dispatch. ICLAttention.q_projection is a standalone bias-free nn.Linear evaluated once per ICL block (~24/predict). Unlike the fused k/v projection (routed through _fused_projection_weight) and the already-accepted out-projection cast cache, its frozen fp32 weight is re-cast to the activation dtype on EVERY call under autocast+inference_mode (autocast does not cache the cast) — a tiny aten::copy_ (the #1 GPU op) per block in this launch/bandwidth-bound regime. Add a single-weight cast cache (_cast_proj_weight, the lone-projection analogue of _fused_projection_weight) keyed on (weight._version, dtype, device) on the module __dict__, and call F.linear with the already-cast frozen weight so autocast no-ops. Bit-identical: F.linear(x, weight.to(dtype)) with bias=False is exactly what autocast would compute (same round-to-nearest cast, same bias-free GEMM); only the redundant per-call cast copy is elided. Gated to not-training / not-compiling and only when a cast is actually needed (returns None otherwise, falling back to the original plain Linear path with identical graph), so the compiled sample path is byte-identical. Mechanism: ~24 fewer copy kernels per typical-path predict.
